title: Project connects old trails to form 1-mile loop in historic Syracuse park
summary: Thornden Park Association and city parks staff launched a pilot project to connect existing brick and paved pathways into a 1-mile loop using inexpensive mulched trails. Three trail sections are planned for 2025, with the first 500-foot section installed in July. The test approach allows the city to gauge public interest before investing in permanent infrastructure like lighting and signage.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Resident-initiated idea (Lawson family) elevated through preservation board and adopted by city parks department, demonstrating responsive governance to neighborhood input.

processing_notes: Article references I-81 director ultimatum and police headquarters project in sidebar but those are distinct articles; only Thornden Park project is primary content
No budget figures provided; project framed as low-cost pilot using donated materials
National Register listing suggests historic preservation compliance considerations
Contact email provided for volunteer engagement (thorndenpark@gmail.com)
